summary: This brief educational article outlines the lasting legacies of several ancient Greek figures. Regarding Socrates, it highlights his lifelong pursuit of truth through questioning and his development of the Socratic method, a pedagogical technique that favors asking thought-provoking questions over lecturing to foster critical thinking.

principles.statement: The search for truth is best conducted through continuous questioning.
principles.rationale: Asking questions helps to elicit the underlying presumptions and ideas that influence how one views a situation.
principles.source_quote: Socrates spent the majority of his life asking questions, always in search of the truth.

frameworks.name: Socratic method
frameworks.when_to_apply: When teaching students or attempting to help someone think critically about a subject or case.
steps: Ask a series of thought-provoking questions.
Use the questions to elicit underlying presumptions and ideas influencing the student's views.
frameworks.source_quote: Instead of lecturing the students, professors will ask them a series of thought-provoking questions. These questions help the students think critically, and they are meant to elicit underlying presumptions and ideas that could be influencing the way a student views a case.

anti_patterns.description: Lecturing students instead of asking them questions.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to help students think critically and does not expose the underlying presumptions influencing their views.
